question: Does the overall image resemble a vintage nautical chart with a yellowed, aged paper texture?
Are the colors low-saturation and muted (e.g., faded inks), avoiding vivid photorealistic tones?
Is the geographical shape of the coastline clearly preserved from the first input image?
Is the sea area rendered as a flat chart fill or wash rather than a photorealistic water surface?
Is the compass positioned in the lower-left corner of the chart?
Are the compass features (star points and N/S/E/W markers) clearly recognizable?
Has the compass texture been transformed from 'chalk on blackboard' to 'ink lines on paper'?
Is the original black background of the compass completely removed?
Does the tentacle appear to emerge partially from the sea area of the chart?
Is the tentacle rendered in an embossed impression or woodcut print style, avoiding photorealistic gloss/slime?
Are the tentacle's suction cups distinct and recognizable within the illustration style?
Do the inserted elements (compass and tentacle) share the same paper grain/texture as the map background?
Is the image free from unnatural rectangular borders or sharp cut-out edges around the inserted elements?
Is the image free from severe blurriness, geometric distortions, or unrequested objects?
